raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Auditor conducted a comprehensive audit of the Santa Clara County Valley Transportation Authority (VTA).",
    "VTA’s process for appointing directors lacks appropriate transparency.",
    "The short length of terms that VTA board members serve is an outlier among similar transit agencies.",
    "The short terms serve as an unnecessary obstacle to board members developing the expertise needed for high-quality oversight.",
    "The State Auditor's audit confirmed the need for changes to VTA’s governance structure.",
    "VTA has rejected the Auditor’s recommendations to improve governance.",
    "VTA’s operating performance has deteriorated over the last 10 years relative to its historical performance and that of its peers.",
    "Many structural barriers to VTA's success have not yet been addressed.",
    "VTA board members report that representatives are often selected based on their interest in higher elected office rather than their familiarity with transportation.",
    "The processes for appointing VTA’s directors are not always transparent enough to ensure the appointment of experienced directors.",
    "VTA’s directors have shorter tenures than those of peer transit agencies due to shorter term lengths established by state law.",
    "VTA needs to strengthen its planning and oversight of capital projects.",
    "VTA does not always estimate the cost to operate and maintain capital projects.",
    "VTA staff do not provide regular updates to the board about cost variances from project estimates.",
    "The construction cost of one project reviewed increased by about 24 percent from the start of construction.",
    "VTA staff misled the board of directors about the true cost of the BART to Silicon Valley project."
  ]
}
```
